title: Source secrets from a managed secrets store at runtime
description: Fetch credentials from a secrets manager with rotation and access control instead of baking them into config files, images, or environment defaults.
body: Use when an application needs database passwords, API keys, or tokens in the cloud. Do not commit secrets to config, images, or IaC, and avoid long-lived static keys where a managed identity works. Actions: store the secret in the cloud secrets manager, grant the workload's identity scoped read access, fetch at runtime (cached with TTL), and enable rotation; record which identity may read which secret. Validation signals include no secret material in source/image scans and an access log showing only the intended identity reading it. Risk boundary: a secret found in config, an image layer, or logs is a disclosure; do not 'fix' by moving it to another plaintext location — rotate the exposed credential and escalate.
